title: anthyllis coccinea
intro: Anthyllis coccinea, also known as kidney vetch, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its clusters of red flowers and small, kidney-shaped seed pods. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In herbal medicine, kidney vetch is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from urinary issues, such as kidney stones and bladder problems, as well as reduced symptoms of menopause and PMS. It can be prepared in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and dried extracts, which are often used to support renal function and overall well-being.
